Land Rover Defender D350 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender D350 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned D350 engine (AJ20D6) typically costs £3,800–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,700. As this is a relatively new engine, used and reconditioned supply is still developing – prices are indicative. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender D350 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is JLR's most powerful diesel, introduced in May 2024. Early data is limited, but it builds on the Ingenium 3.0L diesel platform. A recall has been issued for the FEAD belt idler pulley on certain AJ20-D6 engines – check your VIN. When properly maintained, early reports suggest it's a robust, impressive engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender D350?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issue identified so far is a recall for the Front-End Auxiliary Drive (FEAD) belt idler pulley, which may over-rotate and damage the oil pressure sensor. Other potential issues shared with the Ingenium family include oil filter housing cracking and water pump pulley belt damage – both subject to prior recalls.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender D350 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the Defender D350's high value (typically £60,000–£100,000+), a £4,500–£6,500 reconditioned engine is a small fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. Even at the higher end (£7,500), it rarely exceeds our 60% Land Rover threshold against current market values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The D350 uses the AJ20D6 (also known as DT306) 3.0-litre Ingenium diesel. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The D350 badge itself confirms the engine – it's the only 350PS diesel offered in the L663 Defender.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a D350 engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues identified so far – the FEAD pulley recall, oil filter housing, and water pump belt – are repairs, not full replacements. If caught early, these are straightforward fixes. Major internal failure would require replacement, but there's no widespread evidence of this yet given the engine's recent introduction.

problems.title: FEAD Belt Idler Pulley Issue (AJ20D6 Recall)
problems.symptoms: Unusual noise from front of engine, potential oil leak, warning lights — may affect engine oil pressure and temperature sensors.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall affects 2024–2026 model year vehicles.
problems.repairCost: £0 (if recalled — dealer repair free) / £300–£600 [PLACEHOLDER] (if out of warranty).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (pulley replacement, not full engine replacement).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear unusual front-end noise.

problems.recommendation: A recall has been issued for certain AJ20-D6 engines where the Front-End Auxiliary Drive (FEAD) belt idler pulley may over-rotate following high-torsion input. This can cause the pulley to clash with and damage the engine oil pressure and temperature sensor. Check your VIN against the recall database immediately — this is a free dealer fix. If you hear any unusual noise from the front of the engine, book an inspection without delay.

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing Cracking (Diesel Ingenium Family)
problems.symptoms: Oil leak, burning oil smell, oil warning light, visible oil pooling under the vehicle.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: On six-cylinder diesel (D200, D250, D300, D350) vehicles, an incorrect specification of plastic was used in the oil filter housing. This housing may crack over time, allowing oil under pressure to leak and potentially contact hot engine components — which could result in a fire. A recall affected 599 vehicles across 2020–2024 models. Ensure your vehicle's recall status is checked; this is a straightforward but critical fix.

problems.title: Water Pump Pulley Drive Belt Damage (Ingenium Family)
problems.symptoms: Squealing from front of engine, coolant loss, overheating, warning lights.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (belt replacement, not full engine replacement).

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if you hear belt noise.

problems.recommendation: A recall was issued for the water pump pulley drive belt, which may become damaged and detach. This affected 18,151 vehicles across 2020–2023 models. While the D350 is a newer engine and may have revised components, it shares the same Ingenium architecture. If you hear squealing from the front of the engine or notice coolant loss, have the belt inspected promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender D350 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Recall repair is free; out-of-warranty repair still cheaper than engine replacement
Preventive maintenance — cheaper than engine damage from overheating
Full engine replacement often more cost-effective
Viable on higher-value examples; assess overall vehicle condition

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender D350? Check These First
buyIf: * All recalls are confirmed completed (FEAD pulley, oil filter housing)
* Full Land Rover service history with documented oil changes
* The vehicle has done regular longer runs (DPF-friendly mileage)
* Warranty remaining or an extended warranty in place
avoidIf: * No record of recall completion — these are critical safety items
* Any sign of oil leak from the front or underside of the engine
* Poor service history — this engine demands strict maintenance
* Unusual noise from the front of the engine at any RPM

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Defender D350 represents the most powerful diesel engine ever fitted to a Defender. It's a significant step up from the D300, with 350PS and 700Nm of torque. However, as a relatively new engine (introduced May 2024), long-term reliability data is still emerging. Early recalls — particularly the FEAD belt idler pulley issue affecting AJ20-D6 engines— mean buyers should verify recall completion before purchase. When maintained correctly, this is a genuinely impressive, torquey diesel that transforms the Defender's on-road and towing capability.

cta.label: → Compare Defender D350 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender D350 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Does it start cleanly? Any unusual noise from the front of the engine?
2. Service History — Full Land Rover main dealer history with correct oil spec (STJLR.03.5007)
3. Recall Status — FEAD pulley recall completed? Oil filter housing recall completed?
4. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the oil filter housing for any signs of leakage
5. Belt Condition — Any squealing from the front of the engine? (water pump pulley belt)
6. DPF Regeneration — Has the vehicle done regular longer runs? Short journeys accelerate DPF wear
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures or any advisory notes
